summary: Windows stealth installer with HKCU Run-key persistence, process-name disguise, and eval of obfuscated payload
details: On module load, launcher.js (the package's main) sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' to impersonate the Windows RuntimeBroker.exe system process, then writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points at a bundled VBS file (OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs) impersonating a Microsoft OneDrive component. The VBS calls `objShell.Run "node.exe launcher.js", 0, False`, respawning the package's payload with a hidden window at every user login — durable, invisible reboot-survivable execution. The package's `bin` entry (cli/cli.js) similarly shells out via `execSync('wscript "OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs"')` so the node process is launched hidden from the user. launcher.js then reads server.obf.js (~76 KB, obfuscator.io-packed with a 375-entry rotating string array and _0x-style identifiers) and passes its contents to `eval()` at module top-level, defeating any reviewer inspection of the actual logic. The package additionally ships sens.node, a 6.8 MB Windows PE native addon containing strings consistent with a game cheat ('Head', 'LeftHand', 'LeftFoot', 'Teleport', 'Aimbot') and a public/index.html license-key UI advertising 'Aim/Trigger/Aimbot/Visuals/PlayerList/Vehicle Spawn' panels — contradicting the package's claim to be a benign mobile control panel. Any developer who installs and loads this package executes unaudited native code plus an obfuscated JS payload, with persistence and process disguise specifically engineered to hide that execution from them.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
